{"key_event":"Cultural Revolution and PLA restoration of control","reasoning":"Civil society remained atomized: Red Guard and factional mobilization briefly showed collective capacity, but it was Mao-licensed, violent, unstable, and then crushed or absorbed by PLA-led Revolutionary Committees. State power fell somewhat from the late-1950s peak because famine and Cultural Revolution chaos damaged regular administration, but communes, hukou, work units, militarization, nuclear capability, and PLA intervention left the state still overwhelmingly able to penetrate and coerce society.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":1.3,"state_change":-0.5,"state_power":8.3}
